title: V1 — Stream Claude response → pipe sentences to TTS immediately
description:
File: apps/server/src/routes/claude.routes.ts line 437+

The /api/claude/message endpoint streams Claude output and pipes complete sentences to TTS via speakAndEmit() as they arrive, rather than waiting for the full response. This eliminates perceived latency on voice responses — the user hears the first sentence while Claude is still generating the rest.

Key mechanics already scaffolded:
- extractSentences() splits on . ! ? boundaries with 8-char minimum
- AbortController (V5) cancels in-flight stream on barge-in (jarvis:user_speaking)
- Agentic loop (V9b) re-enters for tool calls
- jarvis:chime fires before Claude call (V9)

Remaining / validation work:
- Confirm sentence extraction handles all edge cases (ellipses, abbreviations, code blocks)
- Verify barge-in abort correctly drains the TTS queue and resets speechSequence
- Ensure speakAndEmit errors (TTS failures) don't stall the stream loop
- Validate seq numbers stay monotonic across a full multi-sentence response
- End-to-end latency test: time from user speech → first audio chunk
